gender.default: Gender-related cues may bias your evaluation. Focus only on job-relevant qualifications, skills, experience, and role requirements.
gender.male: Male-coded cues may bias assumptions about leadership, technical competence, or role fit. Evaluate only resume evidence and job criteria.
gender.female: Female-coded cues may bias assumptions about leadership, technical competence, commitment, or role fit. Evaluate only resume evidence and job criteria.

ethnicity.default: Ethnicity-related cues may bias your evaluation. Do not infer language ability, cultural fit, nationality, or competence from a name or identity cue.
ethnicity.majority: Majority-group name cues may create familiarity bias. Apply the same job-relevant criteria used for all candidates.
ethnicity.minority: Minority-group name cues may create ethnicity-based bias. Do not infer cultural fit, language ability, nationality, or competence from the candidate name.

age.default: Age-related cues may bias your evaluation. Do not infer adaptability, ambition, reliability, or ability from graduation year or career-timeline cues.
age.younger: Younger-age cues may bias assumptions about maturity, reliability, or experience. Evaluate only demonstrated job-relevant evidence.
age.older: Older-age cues may bias assumptions about adaptability, ambition, technology use, or long-term fit. Evaluate only demonstrated job-relevant evidence.

combined: Intersectional combinations of gender, ethnicity, and age cues may bias evaluation. Check that the score and recommendation are justified only by job-relevant evidence.
